headlines.title: Starmer tells of 'intensely personal' decision to quit and warns Burnham in first interview since resigning
headlines.description: In an untypically candid interview, the outgoing PM tells the BBC it was "really tough" to accept his political career was over.

headlines.title: England-Mexico kick-off unchanged at 1am after Fifa U-turn
headlines.description: England's World Cup last-16 tie against Mexico will kick off as scheduled at 01:00 BST on Monday.

headlines.title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce in NYC ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
headlines.description: The star-studded celebration saw several streets in Midtown Manhattan shut, as hordes of fans gathered outside the arena.

headlines.title: In pictures: Stars and fans celebrate Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
headlines.description: Celebrities and sports stars were spotted as the A-list couple tied the knot in New York on Friday.

headlines.title: The massive scramble behind the scenes before the new PM arrives
headlines.description: As Starmer gets ready to pack his bags, there's a "bunfight for jobs" in Whitehall, one MP tells Laura Kuenssberg.

headlines.title: Fireworks, flyovers and a 'really long' Trump speech ahead as US celebrates 250th
headlines.description: The celebrations in the US capital will feature 850,000 fireworks, a "really long" speech from the president and scorching temperatures.

headlines.title: Mini-marts linked to convicted people smuggler closed after BBC investigation
headlines.description: We traced Twana Jamal, once the "the godfather" of French migrant camps, to two shops in Leicestershire.

headlines.title: 'Hotter and hotter and hotter' - Europe's new climate in seven charts
headlines.description: Temperature records have tumbled across the UK and Europe as scientists warn it is a sign of things to come.

headlines.title: What could new rights for unmarried couples mean for your money?
headlines.description: Co-habiting couples in England and Wales could be given more protections if they break up under new government proposals.

headlines.title: The schools starting late after 1am England kick-off to 'reduce pressure' on parents
headlines.description: Some schools are opting to open late, arguing they are striking the right balance, and many parents have backed the decision.

headlines.title: Russia looks to students to make up for mounting losses in Ukraine
headlines.description: Moscow's campaign for students to sign army contracts comes as the Kremlin seeks to sustain its war effort for a fifth year.

headlines.title: How to walk 30 minutes a day - your tips after NHS reward scheme announced
headlines.description: From walking the dog before breakfast to getting off the bus a stop early, readers share their tips for building a daily walking habit.

headlines.title: Five and a half hours that left England's last-16 tie in chaos
headlines.description: For several hours on Friday, no-one seemed to know when England's World Cup last-16 match against co-hosts Mexico would kick off. BBC Sport tells how a story of confusion and anger - culminating in a Fifa U-turn - unfolded.

headlines.title: England team booed on arrival at Mexico City hotel
headlines.description: England are booed as they arrive at their Mexico City hotel to prepare for their World Cup last-16 game against Mexico.

headlines.title: Eight of the 10 most populous countries are not in the World Cup
headlines.description: Many countries with massive populations consistently miss out on football’s biggest party. What can they do to change their sporting fortunes?

headlines.title: Farewell Cape Verde - defeat by Argentina but they are the underdogs the World Cup will never forget
headlines.description: Cape Verde may have been the World Cup's second-smallest nation but they leave the largest impact as they depart following defeat by defending champions Argentina in an epic.

headlines.title: Murals, flags and beef dishes - Messi mania takes hold in Miami
headlines.description: Lionel Messi's World Cup goals roadshow continued with his seventh of the tournament for Argentina against Cape Verde - and delighted the fans who were in Miami to see their hero.

headlines.title: BBC offers 'Stay Up or Catch Up' for England v Mexico
headlines.description: The BBC will be launching a special 'Stay Up or Catch Up' offer for its live coverage of England's game against Mexico at the 2026 World Cup.

headlines.title: UK temperatures forecast to exceed 30C as another heatwave imminent
headlines.description: Another heatwave is on its way and set to last significantly longer as Stav Danaos explains.

headlines.title: China's internet got so mad about a celebrity's 'bad' singing, her concert was cancelled
headlines.description: Celebrities have become outlets for young people's social and economic frustrations.

headlines.title: Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February
headlines.description: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.

headlines.title: Force bosses to face MPs over maternity scandal, says Streeting
headlines.description: Former health secretary Wes Streeting says the "cowardice" of leaders who refused to co-operate was "an insult".

headlines.title: Trump's new take on 250 years of American expansionism
headlines.description: The US has grown, both in terms of its land and its people, over the last 250 years. What's next?

headlines.title: The Papers: Storm threat to England match and 'bid to block Miliband'
headlines.description: Shifting start times at the Fifa World Cup are the lead story for the papers on Saturday.

headlines.title: Ear plugs have become a festival must-have - but they aren't all created equal
headlines.description: Ear plugs are becoming an increasingly popular accessory at music festivals.

headlines.title: 'Flamingo Revolution' takes off as thousands demand Albanian PM's resignation
headlines.description: Flamingos have become a national symbol of protest, triggered by opposition to a luxury development.

headlines.title: Tartan Army's World Cup traffic cone high jinks are taking over Scotland
headlines.description: Scotland fans' coning of statues during the World Cup has led to a surge in the practice back home, but not everyone has welcomed it.

headlines.title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
headlines.description: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

headlines.title: Anguished families left to identify Venezuela quake victims at makeshift morgue
headlines.description: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

headlines.title: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
headlines.description: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out the scene of the attack.

headlines.title: 'I love my superhero legs', says boy who 'inspires everyone around him'
headlines.description: The school says the young double amputee's courage and positivity has "inspired everyone".

headlines.title: Greyhound who refuses to race finds new home in US
headlines.description: Kiki, who was bred to race, will cross the Atlantic to live in California with her new family.

headlines.title: Pupils perform for Foo Fighters in stadium show
headlines.description: The Bell Tower Beats, from Anfield Road Primary School, performed for the US stars in Liverpool.

headlines.title: Mr Blobby costume sells for £8,500 at auction
headlines.description: Auctioneers say they had bids from around the world for the costume of the 'iconic' TV character.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The Story Of Andy Burnham's Return To Westminster - Live At Crossed Wires Festival
headlines.description: Chris and Faisal look at how he's gone from mayor to likely next PM.

headlines.title: After 250 years, do US citizens still believe in the American Dream?
headlines.description: Historian Heather Cox Richardson on what history tells us about modern America

headlines.title: Zendaya stars in this heated tennis love triangle
headlines.description: A love triangle forms between three tennis players.

headlines.title: Five and a half hours that left England's World Cup tie in chaos
headlines.description: For several hours on Friday, no-one seemed to know when England's World Cup last-16 match against co-hosts Mexico would kick off. BBC Sport tells how a story of confusion and anger - culminating in a Fifa U-turn - unfolded.

headlines.title: England team booed on arrival at Mexico City hotel
headlines.description: England are booed as they arrive at their Mexico City hotel to prepare for their World Cup last-16 game against Mexico.

headlines.title: Farewell Cape Verde - the underdogs the World Cup will never forget
headlines.description: Cape Verde may have been the World Cup's second-smallest nation but they leave the largest impact as they depart following defeat by defending champions Argentina in an epic.

headlines.title: All Blacks beat France in thrilling Nations Championship opener
headlines.description: Will Jordan scores twice to help New Zealand beat France as the inaugural Nations Championship opens with a captivating, nine-try thriller in Christchurch.

headlines.title: Argentina overcome Cape Verde in extra-time thriller
headlines.description: Argentina overcome a huge scare as they beat Cape Verde 3-2 in extra time thanks to a deflected header from Cristian Romero to advance to the last 16.
